rulings.Case Title: PETER PEDRETTI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 15, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 583.310, 583.360 is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to plaintiff Paul Pedretti, Jr. Defendant properly served Paul Pedretti, Jr. with the motion and no opposition was filed. The motion is denied without prejudice as to plaintiff Peter Pedretti. Peter remains a plaintiff with Brayton Purcell as his counsel of record however defendant's proof of service failed to demonstrate it provided Peter, through his counsel, notice of its motion. Hearing required unless defendant informs Paul Pedretti, Jr. of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)
